title: haplopappus croceus subsp. typicus
intro: Haplopappus croceus subsp. typicus, also known as the Orange Haplopappus or Yellow Flowered Haplopappus, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. It has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture for herbal use, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal treatment.
